steps.title: Put equations in standard form
steps.content:
Rewrite both equations with $x$ and $y$ on the left:
$$
48x-64y=48y+24
$$
becomes
$$
48x-112y=24
$$
and $ry=\frac{1}{8}-12x$ becomes
$$
12x+ry=\frac{1}{8}
$$

steps.title: Match the parallel coefficients
steps.content:
For no solution, the lines must be parallel and distinct. The $x$-coefficient in the second equation is $\frac{1}{4}$ of the first:
$$
12=\frac{1}{4}(48)
$$
So the $y$-coefficient must use the same factor.

steps.title: Solve for r
steps.content: Apply the same factor to the first equation's $y$-coefficient: $$ r=\frac{1}{4}(-112)=-28 $$ The constants are not in that same ratio, so the lines are distinct. The answer is -28.
